global.title: Carvana’s bet on Slate is ACTUALLY a bet on itself, as the future of automotive retail
global.summary: As new electric vehicle brands like Scout and Slate search for alternatives to franchise auto dealers, America’s largest online auto retailer is quietly positioning itself to become the future of automotive retail. more…

global.title: Wallbox deploys its first Supernova PowerRing DC fast chargers
global.summary: Wallbox just installed its first public Supernova PowerRing DC fast chargers at Port de Sitges, a busy marina on Spain’s Mediterranean coast near Barcelona. more…

global.title: T1 Energy buys KORE Power to cash in on the AI power boom
global.summary: T1 Energy, formerly known as FREYR Battery, is buying battery storage and software company KORE Power in a deal aimed at growing its presence in the fast-expanding energy storage and AI data center markets. more…

global.title: Volkswagen’s push for more affordable EVs starts with these two
global.summary: The first Volkswagen ID. Polo and Cupra Raval models rolled off the production line at the Group’s Martorell plant in Spain, the first of several new affordable, mass-market EVs. more…

global.title: BYD’s ‘world’s first’ 1,000-km range luxury GT is off to a hot start
global.summary: The new Denza Z9 GT can drive over 1,000 km (621 miles) without stopping, fully recharge in under 10 minutes, and starts at about $40,000 in China. Now, BYD is unleashing it in Europe, the UK, and several other markets. more…

global.title: Tesla pushes Roadster demo to August as SpaceX thruster work continues
global.summary: Tesla has pushed the planned public demo of its next-generation Roadster to August or later, according to a report from The Information citing four people with knowledge of the program.
